name_en: BSc (Hons) in Nursing
name_zh: 護理學(榮譽)理學士
short_description: Our mission is to educate young people to pursue professional careers in nursing, one of the world's most caring professions. This innovative academic programme develops students' competency in delivering quality care, carrying out relevant research and making significant contributions to the health of the community. Students master the skills of clinical reasoning, communication and the use of technology. They also learn how to work efficiently alone, or with other healthcare professionals, to provide quality healthcare. Remarks: Students will be awarded the following award upon successful completion of the graduation requirements: BSc (Hons) in Nursing New Global Nursing Leader Distinction for high achieving students starting from 2025/26 admission With the highest DSE scores, students will join the Global Nursing Leader Distinction while they are studying the programme. Special features for students who join this programme: Participate in WHO Collaborating Centres and other global exposure: One internship or study trip in WHO Collaborating Centers in Europe, North America, or Australia Up to two exchanges, including clinical visits and other exposures in Europe, North America, or Australia Support for advanced nursing pathway during undergraduate study: Sponsorship for up to six transferable credits (two subjects) to study in the Master of Science in Nursing programme at PolyU Special mentoring by both senior staff and alumni to shed light on the promotional paths for nursing as clinicians, academics, and entrepreneurs Other credentials: Certificate of Global Nursing Leader Distinction upon completion Certificate in specialisation in Traditional Chinese Medicinal Nursing (TCM) and the opportunity to practice TCM in North America Eligibility to join the programme: Students with the highest DSE scores in the programme Maintain a minimum cGPA of 3.2 throughout the study
contacts_text: General Office of the School of Nursing Email: sn.enquiry@polyu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2766 4369

requirements.raw_text: Requirements Programme Entrance Requirements General Entrance Requirements Same as General Entrance Requirements For admission to the BSc (Hons) in Nursing, students must have Level 3 or above in at least two HKDSE elective subjects from Category A. Please refer to www.hkeaa.edu.hk/en/hkdse/assessment/subject_information/ for details of the HKDSE subject categories. There is no compulsory subject requirement. Preferred subjects with the highest weighting for admission score calculation include: Chinese Language English Language Mathematics Biology Combined Science: Biology + Chemistry Combined Science: Biology + Physics Preference will be given to those who are fluent in written English and Chinese, and in oral English and Cantonese. For applicants with the same admission score, preference will be given to those who have studied the Applied Learning subject, Health Care Practice. Students must pass a health check before clinical training. name_en: BEng (Hons) in Civil Engineering
name_zh: 土木工程學(榮譽)工學士
short_description: This BEng (Hons) in Civil Engineering aims to nurture well-rounded civil engineering graduates who are conversant in environmental, geotechnical, hydraulic, structural, and transportation engineering, as well as construction management. Graduates will be able to plan, design, construct, and manage interdisciplinary civil engineering projects incorporating complex commercial, environmental, social, and legal considerations. Full accreditation has been granted to this programme by The Hong Kong Institution of Engineers (HKIE). Graduates of the programme are qualified for Corporate Membership of HKIE after appropriate training and are eligible to join Scheme A training of HKIE in the civil, environmental, geotechnical, structural, logistics & transportation disciplines. All admitted students will embark on a Common Year One curriculum. Towards the end of year one, students can decide whether to continue with their initial choice or switch to another choice within the Faculty. Those who wish to change to another choice need to join a competitive process through a ranking assessment, which considers a combination of entrance qualification score, Year One GPA, and interview performance. Remarks: Entry Scholarship To recognise those who achieved outstanding academic performance in the HKDSE, the Department will grant entry scholarships to eligible HKDSE students admitted to the programmes via JUPAS. The award criteria of the scholarship listed in this information sheet are subject to change and will be announced on the same website in due course.
contacts_text: Mr Andy Mok of the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering Email: chi-ho-andy.mok@polyu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2766 4482 Ms Kristy Kwan of the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering Email: kristytk.kwan@polyu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2766 6017

No penalty is applied to applicants with multiple attempts in HKDSE Examination. Similar subjects are considered once and the best grade will be taken into consideration during admission selection.

There is no compulsory subject requirement for our schemes / programmes. Preferred subjects, if applicable, are listed in the 'Entrance Requirements & Subject Weightings' section of individual scheme / programme entries. Satisfactory performance in preferred subjects will have a positive influence on admission selection. However, applicants who have not taken the preferred subjects will still be considered for admission but they may need to take relevant underpinning subjects after admission to PolyU.

The study places offered by PolyU through JUPAS are intended primarily for admission of local school leavers. Applicants who are studying or have studied a scheme / programme with degree or above level will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Please note that local and non-local applicants are considered under different admission routes with different intake quotas. Non-local applicants seeking admission on the basis of HKDSE results should submit their applications directly to PolyU. Please click here for details on the admission requirements for non-local applicants.

Applicants who fall short slightly on the general entrance requirements of our schemes / programmes but meet the following criteria may be specially considered for admission: with one HKDSE subject result falling one level below the required level, except "Citizenship and Social Development" (or "Liberal Studies" for repeaters), but attained with a flexible admission threshold score of the scheme / programme concerned (Please click here for the threshold score); and have selected PolyU’s degree programmes as their Band A choices, i.e. first 3 priorities, in JUPAS Note: This consideration does not apply to JS3000 Bachelor’s Degree Scheme in Interdisciplinary Studies.

name_en: BEng (Hons) Scheme in Mechanical Engineering (Intelligent Robotics Engineering / Mechanical Engineering)
name_zh: 機械工程學(榮譽)工學士組合課程 (智能機械人工程學 / 機械工程學)
short_description: Updated Students admitted to the Scheme will study together for the first 2 years, and then complete their preferred programme normally within the next two years until graduation. Free selection (with no quota limit) of either one programme for Years 3 and 4: BEng (Hons) in Intelligent Robotics Engineering 智能機械人工程學 (榮譽)工學士 Intelligent robotics is an emerging engineering discipline that advances many systems on which modern society relies, including autonomous manufacturing systems, automated consumer products, automated experimental systems, intelligent vehicles, Internet of Things (IoT), healthcare, medical devices, and many more. The BEng (Hons) in Intelligent Robotics Engineering programme is the first of its kind in Hong Kong which aims to educate highly skilled engineers in the field of intelligent robotics to local and global industries. BEng (Hons) in Mechanical Engineering 機械工程學(榮譽)工學士 We nurture mechanical engineering graduates who are able to apply modern technology to solve and manage multidisciplinary engineering problems. They have excellent employment opportunities in many industries/businesses, and are able to start their careers as mechanical engineers, service engineers, consultant engineers, design engineers or project engineers. Secondary Major in AI and Data Analytics 「人工智能及數據分析」副主修課程 Mechanical engineering combined with AI knowledge offers a unique and highly valued skill set for students. They can create innovative products, analyse data, and optimise performance, making them valuable in industries such as automotive, aerospace, and robotics. This integrated discipline offers great career opportunities for those interested in cutting-edge technologies. Admission to a Secondary Major is on a competitive basis and subject to different credit requirements for graduation. All admitted students will embark on a Common Year One curriculum. Towards the end of year one, students can decide whether to continue with their initial choice or switch to another choice within the Faculty of Engineering. Those who wish to change to another choice need to join a competitive process through a ranking assessment, which considers a combination of entrance qualification score, Year One GPA, and interview performance. Remarks: The Bachelor of Engineering (Honours) Scheme in Mechanical Engineering includes the following two awards: BEng (Hons) in Intelligent Robotics Engineering 智能機械人工程學 (榮譽)工學士 BEng (Hons) in Mechanical Engineering 機械工程學(榮譽)工學士 Students are allowed to enrol on any awards without being subject to further assessment. Entry Scholarship To recognise applicants with outstanding academic performance in the HKDSE, the Department will grant entry scholarships to eligible HKDSE students admitted to its programmes via JUPAS. Please refer https://www.polyu.edu.hk/me/study/scholarship-and-financial-assistance/ for further details.
contacts_text: Prof. Randolph Leung of the Department of Mechanical Engineering Email: randolph.leung@polyu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2766 6645 Prof. Zheng Guangping of the Department of Mechanical Engineering Email: g.zheng@polyu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2766 6660 General Office of the Department of Mechanical Engineering Email: me.enquiry@polyu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2766 6657

name_en: BSc (Hons) in Surveying
name_zh: 地產及建設測量學(榮譽)理學士
short_description: This challenging programme aims to develop all-round and flexible graduates with fundamental knowledge and practical skills in the interdisciplinary professions of land management, real estate development and construction project management.  The Programme underpins surveying studies with the disciplines of economics and finance, law, management and technology and cultivates a global mindset with international studies in the real estate and construction markets in China and overseas. Students are well prepared to enter the professions of building surveying, general practice surveying (real estate), planning and development, property and facility management and quantity surveying (construction economics) with full potentials to take leading and strategic roles in the profession and business of land, property and construction in future. All admitted students will embark on a Common Year One curriculum. Towards the end of year one, students can decide whether to continue with their initial choice or switch to another choice within the Faculty. Those who wish to change to another choice need to join a competitive process through a ranking assessment, which considers a combination of entrance qualification score, Year One GPA, and interview performance. Remarks: Students will be awarded the following upon successful completion of the graduation requirements: BSc(Hons) in Surveying To recognise high academic attainment, the Department will grant entry scholarships to eligible students with outstanding HKDSE scores admitted via JUPAS.
contacts_text: General Office of the Department of Building and Real Estate Email: bsyywu@polyu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 3400 8121 / 3400 8120 / 3400 3819

name_en: BSc (Hons) in Building Engineering and Management
name_zh: 建築工程及管理學(榮譽)理學士
short_description: This programme provides an excellent opportunity for students who would like to pursue an education in the building engineering and construction management profession. The advancing building construction industry in Hong Kong and neighbouring regions requires graduates who possess highly specialised skills in technology, management and communication. Students also face intellectual challenges along the course of study that promote self-development and enhance problem-solving skills. Our well-trained graduates are in great demand as professional building engineers and construction managers because they can easily adapt to the changing needs of the construction industry and have developed the expertise that is necessary to plan, coordinate, supervise and control the complex process of building projects. All admitted students will embark on a Common Year One curriculum. Towards the end of year one, students can decide whether to continue with their initial choice or switch to another choice within the Faculty. Those who wish to change to another choice need to join a competitive process through a ranking assessment, which considers a combination of entrance qualification score, Year One GPA, and interview performance. Remarks: Students will be awarded the following upon successful completion of the graduation requirements: BSc (Hons) in Building Engineering & Management The option of Secondary Major in Artificial Intelligence and Data Analytics (AIDA) is available to the students of BSc (Hons) in Building Engineering and Management. Admission to the Secondary Majors is on a competitive basis and is subject to a different credit requirement for graduation. To recognise high academic attainment, the Department will grant entry scholarships to eligible students with outstanding HKDSE scores admitted via JUPAS.
contacts_text: General Office of the Department of Building and Real Estate Email: bsyywu@polyu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 3400 8121 / 3400 8120 / 3400 3819

No penalty is applied to applicants with multiple attempts in HKDSE Examination. Similar subjects are considered once and the best grade will be taken into consideration during admission selection.

There is no compulsory subject requirement for our schemes / programmes. Preferred subjects, if applicable, are listed in the 'Entrance Requirements & Subject Weightings' section of individual scheme / programme entries. Satisfactory performance in preferred subjects will have a positive influence on admission selection. However, applicants who have not taken the preferred subjects will still be considered for admission but they may need to take relevant underpinning subjects after admission to PolyU.

The study places offered by PolyU through JUPAS are intended primarily for admission of local school leavers. Applicants who are studying or have studied a scheme / programme with degree or above level will be considered on a case-by-case basis.

Please note that local and non-local applicants are considered under different admission routes with different intake quotas. Non-local applicants seeking admission on the basis of HKDSE results should submit their applications directly to PolyU. Please click here for details on the admission requirements for non-local applicants.

Applicants who fall short slightly on the general entrance requirements of our schemes / programmes but meet the following criteria may be specially considered for admission: with one HKDSE subject result falling one level below the required level, except "Citizenship and Social Development" (or "Liberal Studies" for repeaters), but attained with a flexible admission threshold score of the scheme / programme concerned (Please click here for the threshold score); and have selected PolyU’s degree programmes as their Band A choices, i.e. first 3 priorities, in JUPAS Note: This consideration does not apply to JS3000 Bachelor’s Degree Scheme in Interdisciplinary Studies.

name_en: BSc (Hons) Scheme in Computing and AI (Computer Science / Enterprise Information Systems)
name_zh: 電子計算及人工智能(榮譽)理學士組合課程 (計算機科學 / 企業信息管理)
short_description: Updated The Scheme aims to nurture talents and professional to support the fast evolution of the IT industry and advance the frontiers of knowledge. Upon completion of the first year of study, students may choose one of the following majors options below: The BSc (Hons) in Computer Science [計算機科學(榮譽)理學士] aims to equip graduates with solid computing foundations in areas such as programming, computational thinking, data structures and algorithms, and become highly competent in software development for virtually all sectors. The BSc (Hons) in Enterprise Information Systems [企業信息管理(榮譽)理學士] aims to provide graduates with strong competence in investigating business systems, identifying improvement options and bridging the needs of business with the use of IT. All admitted students will embark on a Common Year One curriculum. Towards the end of year one, students can decide whether to continue with their initial choice or switch to another choice within the Faculty of Computer and Mathematical Sciences. Those who wish to change to another choice need to join a competitive process through a ranking assessment, which considers a combination of entrance qualification score, Year One GPA, and interview performance. Remarks: Students will receive one of the following awards upon completion of the programme: BSc (Hons) in Computer Science BSc (Hons) in Enterprise Information Systems Students are allowed to enrol on any awards without being subject to further assessment. Students may also choose to pursue a Double Major, adding a Second Major from another department within the Faculty of Computer and Mathematical Sciences to their studies.  This option gives students the chance to broaden their academic experience and earn two awards when they graduate. Admission to the Double Major is competitive and requires a different credit requirement for graduation.
contacts_text: General Office of the Department of Computing Email: comp.ug@polyu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2766 7300

name_en: Bachelor of Social Sciences with Honours
name_zh: 社會科學榮譽學士
short_description: The BSSc(Hons) is a 4-year full-time programme aiming to equip students with in-depth knowledge in a wide range of social science disciplines, critical and analytical thinking skills, and the abilities to conduct effective social research and serve the community in action. Through majoring in different social science streams, students learn specialised knowledge and practical skills in their areas of study. The programme is structured to also develop problem-solving skills, teamwork and peer leadership paving the way for graduates to continue postgraduate studies or to pursue careers in private and public sectors, non-government organisations, cultural institutions, and advocacy groups. The BSSc(Hons) currently offers the following streams: Stream I : Ageing Society and Services Studies Stream II : Applied Economics Stream III : Applied Social Studies Stream IV : Global and China Studies Stream V : Politics and Public Administration JUPAS applicants will be considered for admission to first-year studies at Bachelor of Social Sciences with Honours. Student will enter a stream after year 1 study and follow the respective programme curriculum in Years 2 – 4. Remarks: The Bachelor of Social Sciences with Honours has five streams: Ageing Society and Services Studies, Applied Economics, Applied Social Studies, Global and China Studies, Politics and Public Administration. Student will choose a stream by the end of Year 1 study and then follow the respective programme curriculum.
tuition_fee_full_text: Updated HK$ 84,170 Estimated Programme Fees 1st year: HK$84,170 Whole Programme: HK$336,680 Estimated Programme Fees after deducting NMTSS subsidy (HK$35,120/year), if applicable 1st year: HK$49,050 Whole Programme: HK$196,200 Remarks: The composite tuition/programme fees listed above have included laboratory/practicum/immersion course fees, etc., if applicable. Non-local students will be charged at a higher rate. There will be an annual adjustment of tuition fees which will be capped at the actual Composite Consumer Price Index (CCPI) to be announced by the Government. The estimated composite tuition/programme fees for the whole programme have not yet taken the CCPI for the second to fourth (or fifth) year, i.e. 2027/28 to 2029/30 (or 2030/31) academic year, into account. Students, who fulfilled the stipulated requirements set by the Education Bureau (EDB), are eligible for the Non-means-tested Subsidy Scheme for Self-financing Undergraduate Studies in Hong Kong (“NMTSS”) provided by the Government. For details of the scheme and eligibility, please visit https://www.cspe.edu.hk/en/nmt/ . The NMTSS subsidy amount of HK$35,120/year set out above is for the 2026/27 academic year (AY). The NMTSS subsidy amount for the 2027/28 academic year is to be announced by the EDB. A student’s eligibility for the NMTSS and the annual subsidy amount allocated in an academic year is subject to EDB’s final approval. Only local students are eligible for subsidy under NMTSS.
contacts_text: Dr. Raymond Kwong Email: rkwong@hkmu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2768 5739 Dr. CHAN Wai Leung Email: walchan@hkmu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2768 5737

notes: Requirements
Applicants with previous HKDSE subject result of A040 ‘Liberal Studies’ will be used as an alternative to the core subject requirement of A045 ‘Citizenship and Social Development’.

Unless otherwise stated in the Programme-specific subject requirements, HKDSE Category B subjects (Applied Learning) with attainment at “Attained” or above, or Category C subjects (Other Languages) at specified levels, can be considered as elective subjects, and up to 2 Applied Learning subjects (excluding Applied Learning Chinese) will be recognized as electives.

Mathematics Extended Part (i.e., Module 1 (Calculus and Statistics) / Module 2 (Algebra and Calculus)) will be counted as an elective subject. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, only the module with the best result will be taken into consideration.

Non-Chinese Speaking (NCS) applicants, who have met the specified circumstances as announced by the Education Bureau (EDB), can use the following qualifications to replace HKDSE Chinese Language level 3 to meet the entrance requirement for Chinese Language: - ‘Attained’ or above in HKDSE Category B (Applied Learning Chinese); or - Grade E or above in GCE (A-Level / AS-Level / O-Level) Chinese ; or - Grade E / Grade 3 or above in GCSE / IGCSE Chinese For programmes with specific entrance requirements on the Chinese Language proficiency, please click here for details.
The University accepts combined results of the HKDSE examination. The best results will be considered for meeting the admission requirements and for the calculation of admission scores.
Calculation of scores for applicants with more than 1 sitting in HKDSE Examination
HKMU will consider the best grade attained in a particular subject from any sittings of the HKDSE examination taken by the applicant. There is no grade penalty for multiple sittings.

There is no grade penalty for multiple sittings.

name_en: Bachelor of Social Sciences with Honours in Psychology
name_zh: 心理學榮譽社會科學學士
short_description: The BSSc(Hons) Psychology is a 4-year full-time program designed to equip students with a broad and comprehensive understanding of human behavior across diverse fields of psychology. Students gain theoretical knowledge and practical skills by specializing in one of two streams: Psychology or Counselling Psychology. The program aims to develop and enhance students’ abilities in scientific reasoning, critical thinking, and problem-solving, as well as applying psychological principles, selecting effective treatment strategies, and utilizing counselling techniques in real-world situations. Graduates are well-prepared for careers in healthcare, education, social services, and human resources, or for pursuing advanced studies in psychology. The BSSc(Hons)Psychology currently offers two streams: Stream I : Psychology Stream II : Counselling Psychology JUPAS applicants will be considered for admission to first-year studies at Bachelor of Social Sciences with Honours in Psychology. Students will enter a stream after Year 1 study and follow the respective programme curriculum in Years 2 – 4. Remarks: The Bachelor of Social Sciences with Honours in Psychology offers two streams: Psychology and Counselling Psychology. Students will select a stream by the end of their first year of study and then follow the respective program curriculum.
tuition_fee_full_text: Updated HK$ 84,170 Estimated Programme Fees 1st year: HK$84,170 Whole Programme: HK$336,680 Estimated Programme Fees after deducting NMTSS subsidy (HK$35,120/year), if applicable 1st year: HK$49,050 Whole Programme: HK$196,200 Remarks: The composite tuition/programme fees listed above have included laboratory/practicum/immersion course fees, etc., if applicable. Non-local students will be charged at a higher rate. There will be an annual adjustment of tuition fees which will be capped at the actual Composite Consumer Price Index (CCPI) to be announced by the Government. The estimated composite tuition/programme fees for the whole programme have not yet taken the CCPI for the second to fourth (or fifth) year, i.e. 2027/28 to 2029/30 (or 2030/31) academic year, into account. Students, who fulfilled the stipulated requirements set by the Education Bureau (EDB), are eligible for the Non-means-tested Subsidy Scheme for Self-financing Undergraduate Studies in Hong Kong (“NMTSS”) provided by the Government. For details of the scheme and eligibility, please visit https://www.cspe.edu.hk/en/nmt/ . The NMTSS subsidy amount of HK$35,120/year set out above is for the 2026/27 academic year (AY). The NMTSS subsidy amount for the 2027/28 academic year is to be announced by the EDB. A student’s eligibility for the NMTSS and the annual subsidy amount allocated in an academic year is subject to EDB’s final approval. Only local students are eligible for subsidy under NMTSS.
contacts_text: Dr. Vera HAU Email: vhau@hkmu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 2768 5860

notes: Requirements
Applicants with previous HKDSE subject result of A040 ‘Liberal Studies’ will be used as an alternative to the core subject requirement of A045 ‘Citizenship and Social Development’.

Unless otherwise stated in the Programme-specific subject requirements, HKDSE Category B subjects (Applied Learning) with attainment at “Attained” or above, or Category C subjects (Other Languages) at specified levels, can be considered as elective subjects, and up to 2 Applied Learning subjects (excluding Applied Learning Chinese) will be recognized as electives.

Mathematics Extended Part (i.e., Module 1 (Calculus and Statistics) / Module 2 (Algebra and Calculus)) will be counted as an elective subject. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, only the module with the best result will be taken into consideration.

Non-Chinese Speaking (NCS) applicants, who have met the specified circumstances as announced by the Education Bureau (EDB), can use the following qualifications to replace HKDSE Chinese Language level 3 to meet the entrance requirement for Chinese Language: - ‘Attained’ or above in HKDSE Category B (Applied Learning Chinese); or - Grade E or above in GCE (A-Level / AS-Level / O-Level) Chinese ; or - Grade E / Grade 3 or above in GCSE / IGCSE Chinese For programmes with specific entrance requirements on the Chinese Language proficiency, please click here for details.
The University accepts combined results of the HKDSE examination. The best results will be considered for meeting the admission requirements and for the calculation of admission scores.
Calculation of scores for applicants with more than 1 sitting in HKDSE Examination
HKMU will consider the best grade attained in a particular subject from any sittings of the HKDSE examination taken by the applicant. There is no grade penalty for multiple sittings.

name_en: Bachelor of Arts with Honours in Chinese
name_zh: 中文榮譽文學士
short_description: This programme aims to equip students with an in-depth understanding of Chinese language and literature. Training in communication and practical writing skills will be provided to enhance students’ language proficiency. In the area of Chinese literature, this programme will help students to develop the ability of literary criticism. Both classical and modern literary works from a range of genres and relevant literary theories will be covered. Upon graduation, students will be able to engage in the education, government and public administration, culture and media industry. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Arts with Honours in Language Studies and Translation
name_zh: 語言研究與翻譯榮譽文學士
short_description: The Bachelor of Arts with Honours in Language Studies and Translation programme is designed to help students develop practical skills they need in linguistic analysis and translation to succeed at university, work, and beyond. With ongoing guidance and support from a team of approachable teaching staff, students will not only learn a range of linguistic and translation skills, including how to deal with the cultural dimension of translation, account for the differences between the English and Chinese languages, translate texts in specialized areas, machine translation, interpretation, etc., it also offers basic training of the theories in Language Studies and Translation Studies, preparing students for further studies in these fields. Apart from pursuing further studies, our graduates also have ready access to a wealth of career opportunities in professions such as translation, publishing, media, administration, education, etc. Graduates with relevant teacher training can apply for full exemption from the Language Proficiency Requirement for English Teachers. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Arts with Honours in Creative Advertising and Media Design
name_zh: 創意廣告及媒體設計榮譽文學士
short_description: The programme aims to provide students with an in-depth education and training in strategic communications, digital advertising, media design, copywriting, and branding and campaign planning. Media skills, creative thinking and communication design are emphasized. In addition to professional facilities such as photography studio, film/TV production studio, sound recording studio, video editing and design laboratories, and art studio, the programme is taught by scholars and experts of media and advertising fields to equip students with the latest practical knowledge for creative careers. With a Bachelor of Arts in Creative Advertising and Media Design degree, students are well suited for a wide range of careers in the creative media industries such as advertising design, digital and interactive media, strategic planning, art gallery and museum, film/TV production, and media education. Opportunities for postgraduate studies are available in Hong Kong and abroad. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Arts with Honours in English Language and Culture
name_zh: 英語及文化榮譽文學士
short_description: The programme intends to provide students with a broad understanding of the English language through a variety of courses in literary works, linguistics and multimedia texts.  The programme equips students with a solid understanding of the expansion of western culture in the context of globalization. Through the study of English-language literature, popular culture and mass media, students will be equipped with the mastery of English language and knowledge of the cultural framework. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Business Administration with Honours in Professional Accounting
name_zh: 專業會計榮譽工商管理學士
short_description: This programme prepares students for a professional qualification in accounting and equips them with broad-based knowledge in financial accounting, management accounting, taxation, auditing, law and accounting information systems. Graduates can seek employment as professional accountants or pursue careers in related fields such as auditing, taxation and business advisory services. The programme is accredited by the Hong Kong Institute of Certified Public Accountants (HKICPA), the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA), the Association of International Accountants (AIA), and CPA Australia. Graduates are eligible to register with HKICPA's Qualification Programme and claim paper exemptions from examinations of ACCA, AIA and CPA Australia. Remarks: -
tuition_fee_full_text: Updated HK$ 97,730 Estimated Programme Fees 1st year: HK$97,730 Whole Programme: HK$390,920 Estimated Programme Fees after deducting NMTSS subsidy (HK$35,120/year), if applicable 1st year: HK$62,610 Whole Programme: HK$250,440 Remarks: The composite tuition/programme fees listed above have included laboratory/practicum/immersion course fees, etc., if applicable. Non-local students will be charged at a higher rate. There will be an annual adjustment of tuition fees which will be capped at the actual Composite Consumer Price Index (CCPI) to be announced by the Government. The estimated composite tuition/programme fees for the whole programme have not yet taken the CCPI for the second to fourth (or fifth) year, i.e. 2027/28 to 2029/30 (or 2030/31) academic year, into account. Students, who fulfilled the stipulated requirements set by the Education Bureau (EDB), are eligible for the Non-means-tested Subsidy Scheme for Self-financing Undergraduate Studies in Hong Kong (“NMTSS”) provided by the Government. For details of the scheme and eligibility, please visit https://www.cspe.edu.hk/en/nmt/ . The NMTSS subsidy amount of HK$35,120/year set out above is for the 2026/27 academic year (AY). The NMTSS subsidy amount for the 2027/28 academic year is to be announced by the EDB. A student’s eligibility for the NMTSS and the annual subsidy amount allocated in an academic year is subject to EDB’s final approval. Only local students are eligible for subsidy under NMTSS.

notes: Requirements
Applicants with previous HKDSE subject result of A040 ‘Liberal Studies’ will be used as an alternative to the core subject requirement of A045 ‘Citizenship and Social Development’.

Unless otherwise stated in the Programme-specific subject requirements, HKDSE Category B subjects (Applied Learning) with attainment at “Attained” or above, or Category C subjects (Other Languages) at specified levels, can be considered as elective subjects, and up to 2 Applied Learning subjects (excluding Applied Learning Chinese) will be recognized as electives.

Mathematics Extended Part (i.e., Module 1 (Calculus and Statistics) / Module 2 (Algebra and Calculus)) will be counted as an elective subject. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, only the module with the best result will be taken into consideration.

Non-Chinese Speaking (NCS) applicants, who have met the specified circumstances as announced by the Education Bureau (EDB), can use the following qualifications to replace HKDSE Chinese Language level 3 to meet the entrance requirement for Chinese Language: - ‘Attained’ or above in HKDSE Category B (Applied Learning Chinese); or - Grade E or above in GCE (A-Level / AS-Level / O-Level) Chinese ; or - Grade E / Grade 3 or above in GCSE / IGCSE Chinese For programmes with specific entrance requirements on the Chinese Language proficiency, please click here for details.
The University accepts combined results of the HKDSE examination. The best results will be considered for meeting the admission requirements and for the calculation of admission scores.

HKMU will consider the best grade attained in a particular subject from any sittings of the HKDSE examination taken by the applicant. There is no grade penalty for multiple sittings.

name_zh: 商業管理學榮譽工商管理學士

Building on the management knowledge they have acquired in the programme, students can further choose to specialise in a particular field of study, ranging from human resource management, international business, marketing, and supply chain management. The universality of the business management knowledge and skills would provide graduates enhanced career opportunities in a wide range of professions and industries. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Business Administration with Honours in Global Business
name_zh: 環球商業榮譽工商管理學士
short_description: The programme is designed to equip students with global perspectives as well as knowledge and skills necessary to solve problems and make practical recommendations for multinational corporations and organisations doing business internationally. Students enrolled in this programme will develop proficiency in a foreign language and join a one-term exchange at an overseas institution during their study. Through these unique opportunities and experiences, students will not only gain a deeper understanding of the factors shaping businesses in an international context, but also develop soft skills that will help them succeed in diverse teams or multinational organisations. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Business Administration with Honours in Human Resource Management
name_zh: 人力資源管理學榮譽工商管理學士

name_en: Bachelor of Business Administration with Honours in Marketing
name_zh: 市場學榮譽工商管理學士
short_description: The Marketing programme prepares students for diverse and dynamic careers in marketing communications, advertising, public relations, and beyond. The curriculum goes beyond foundational knowledge, incorporating cutting-edge trends and marketing tools such as experiential marketing, digital marketing, and the use of artificial intelligence and big data in e-retailing and marketing analytics. Students will benefit from abundant hands-on practical training, allowing them to apply their skills in real business settings. Graduates will be equipped with professional expertise and advanced tools, empowering them to make strategic, data-driven, and impactful decisions in today’s fast-evolving marketing landscape. Remarks: -

notes: Requirements
Applicants with previous HKDSE subject result of A040 ‘Liberal Studies’ will be used as an alternative to the core subject requirement of A045 ‘Citizenship and Social Development’.

Unless otherwise stated in the Programme-specific subject requirements, HKDSE Category B subjects (Applied Learning) with attainment at “Attained” or above, or Category C subjects (Other Languages) at specified levels, can be considered as elective subjects, and up to 2 Applied Learning subjects (excluding Applied Learning Chinese) will be recognized as electives.

Mathematics Extended Part (i.e., Module 1 (Calculus and Statistics) / Module 2 (Algebra and Calculus)) will be counted as an elective subject. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, only the module with the best result will be taken into consideration.

Non-Chinese Speaking (NCS) applicants, who have met the specified circumstances as announced by the Education Bureau (EDB), can use the following qualifications to replace HKDSE Chinese Language level 3 to meet the entrance requirement for Chinese Language: - ‘Attained’ or above in HKDSE Category B (Applied Learning Chinese); or - Grade E or above in GCE (A-Level / AS-Level / O-Level) Chinese ; or - Grade E / Grade 3 or above in GCSE / IGCSE Chinese For programmes with specific entrance requirements on the Chinese Language proficiency, please click here for details.
The University accepts combined results of the HKDSE examination. The best results will be considered for meeting the admission requirements and for the calculation of admission scores.
Calculation of scores for applicants with more than 1 sitting in HKDSE Examination
HKMU will consider the best grade attained in a particular subject from any sittings of the HKDSE examination taken by the applicant. There is no grade penalty for multiple sittings.

name_en: Bachelor of Applied Psychology with Honours, Bachelor of Business Management with Honours
name_zh: 應用心理學榮譽學士，商業管理榮譽學士
short_description: This dual degree programme is jointly offered by Lee Shau Kee School of Business and Administration and School of Arts and Social Sciences of HKMU. Students will complete 150 credit-units within four years and obtain two honours degrees, one in Applied Psychology, and the other in Business Management. The programme provides students exposure to a wide spectrum of psychological and managerial perspectives during their study. Graduates would be able to integrate psychology and management concepts and theories to develop business solutions that factor in interests, behaviours and mental processes of different stakeholders. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Business Administration with Honours in Sustainable Tourism and Hospitality Management
name_zh: 持續旅遊及款待管理榮譽工商管理學士
short_description: The programme imparts knowledge on general management in the tourism and hospitality sector, with a specific focus on emerging sustainability issues and trends in the sector. The programme also emphasises on experiential learning, as students enjoy various early-industry engagement opportunities to apply what they learn in the classroom to real-world situations. This programme integrates rigorous academic foundations with applied industry experience, empowering graduates to design and implement operational strategies that drive excellence in hospitality management while embedding sustainable practices across the tourism sector. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Business Administration with Honours in Sports and eSports Management
name_zh: 運動及電競運動管理榮譽工商管理學士
short_description: The programme provides a broad-based education in the management of organisations, facilities, and events in traditional sports as well as the fast-growing eSports industry. Students will gain exposure to theoretical knowledge from multiple business disciplines, while gaining extensive hands-on experience through the programme’s work-based learning project. Graduates of this programme would be able to demonstrate knowledge, skills and experience in athletic management as well as sports and eSports management. They would be able to produce practical management or marketing solutions for various organisations and operations related to sports and eSports. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Education with Honours (Putonghua and Chinese Language Education) and Bachelor of Language Studies with Honours (Chinese Linguistic Studies)
name_zh: 教育榮譽學士（普通話及中文教育）及語言研究榮譽學士（漢語語言學研究）
short_description: This programme aims to equip students with ability to analyse the Chinese language, particularly Putonghua, and its practical application in teaching both Putonghua and Chinese Language subjects. Through the inclusion of summer term studies, the programme enables students to obtain two Honours Degrees, completing five years of study in just four. Graduates will possess the necessary professional knowledge and skills to become fully qualified teachers of Putonghua and Chinese in local mainstream as well as international primary and secondary schools. They will be eligible for teacher registration in Hong Kong and potentially internationally. Graduates may pursue various career paths in education, publishing, media, public relations, government, or non-government organizations, leveraging their language proficiency and analytical abilities. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Education with Honours in English Language Teaching and Bachelor of English Language Studies with Honours
name_zh: 英語教學榮譽教育學士及英語研究榮譽學士
short_description: This programme aims to develop a good understanding of the structure and use of the English language, the sound professional knowledge and skills required of a fully qualified English language teacher in both primary and secondary schools. The programme leads to the award of two Honours Degrees - by including studies in two summer terms, the equivalent of five years of studies can be completed within four years. Graduates will be recognized by the Education Bureau as having a language (English) major degree and a teacher qualification specializing in English language, and will be exempted from the English Language Proficiency Requirement (LPR). They will become fully qualified English Language teachers in Hong Kong upon graduation. Graduates will also be eligible to take up general administrative and executive work in the civil service, non-government organizations, the business or industry sectors and other positions for university graduates. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of English Language Studies with Honours
name_zh: 英語研究榮譽學士
short_description: This programme aims to develop a high level of English proficiency and in-depth knowledge and understanding of key aspects in English linguistics and literature. By completing the relevant translation elective courses, students may also graduate with a minor in Translation. The degree is recognized by the Education Bureau as an English language major degree, and graduates who also complete a teacher's qualification specializing in English language will become fully qualified English language teachers in Hong Kong and will be exempted from the English Language Proficiency Assessment for Teachers.  Graduates will be eligible to take up general administrative and executive work in the civil service, non-government organizations, the business or industry sectors and other positions for university graduates. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Education with Honours (Chinese Language Teaching) and Bachelor of Language Studies with Honours (Applied Chinese Language Studies)
name_zh: 教育榮譽學士（中國語文教學）及語言研究榮譽學士（應用中國語言）
short_description: This programme aims to develop in-depth knowledge on the Chinese language and its application in various bilingual working contexts. The programme leads to the award of two Honours Degrees – by including studies in summer terms, the equivalent of five years of studies can be completed within four years – which provide the professional knowledge and skills required of a fully qualified Chinese language teacher for both primary and secondary schools. Graduates will be recognized by the Education Bureau as having a Chinese language major degree and a teacher qualification specializing in Chinese language education. Graduates can also contribute to the fields of publishing and media, creative industries, and general administration in the civil service, non-government organizations and business sectors. name_en: Bachelor of Education with Honours in Early Childhood Education (Leadership and Special Educational Needs)
name_zh: 教育榮譽學士（幼兒教育：領導及特殊教育需要）
short_description: Students will learn theories, analytical frameworks and practices of early childhood education. They will also engage in the teaching of young children in field work practicum.  Upon graduation, they are fully qualified as kindergarten teachers, child care workers, child care centre supervisors, and special childcare workers. They also have met the academic requirements to serve as kindergarten principals. Most courses in the programme use Chinese as medium of instruction. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Science with Honours in Cyber and Computer Security
name_zh: 網路及電腦安全榮譽理學士
short_description: Cyber security is one of the most in-demand skillsets around the world, especially in countries that are developing smart cities. This programme aims to provide students with a breadth of knowledge of theories and professional practices in cyber and computer security. Students will gain an in-depth understanding of the specialized areas of computer network, network security, blockchain, IoT, and digital forensics. Graduates may pursue positions such as network engineer, system analyst, digital forensic expert, and cyber security specialist. Potential employers include mobile / network services providers, and corporations / organizations in various sectors, including government, finance, healthcare, and logistics. Remarks: -
tuition_fee_full_text: Updated HK$ 85,760 Estimated Programme Fees 1st year: HK$85,760 Whole Programme: HK$343,040 Estimated Programme Fees after deducting NMTSS subsidy (HK$35,120/year), if applicable 1st year: HK$50,640 Whole Programme: HK$202,560 Remarks: The composite tuition/programme fees listed above have included laboratory/practicum/immersion course fees, etc., if applicable. Non-local students will be charged at a higher rate. There will be an annual adjustment of tuition fees which will be capped at the actual Composite Consumer Price Index (CCPI) to be announced by the Government. The estimated composite tuition/programme fees for the whole programme have not yet taken the CCPI for the second to fourth (or fifth) year, i.e. 2027/28 to 2029/30 (or 2030/31) academic year, into account. Students, who fulfilled the stipulated requirements set by the Education Bureau (EDB), are eligible for the Non-means-tested Subsidy Scheme for Self-financing Undergraduate Studies in Hong Kong (“NMTSS”) provided by the Government. For details of the scheme and eligibility, please visit https://www.cspe.edu.hk/en/nmt/ . The NMTSS subsidy amount of HK$35,120/year set out above is for the 2026/27 academic year (AY). The NMTSS subsidy amount for the 2027/28 academic year is to be announced by the EDB. A student’s eligibility for the NMTSS and the annual subsidy amount allocated in an academic year is subject to EDB’s final approval. Only local students are eligible for subsidy under NMTSS.
contacts_text: Dr. Farah Yan Email: fyan@hkmu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 3120 2608 Dr. Kevin Hung Email: khung@hkmu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 3120 2603

notes: Requirements
Applicants with previous HKDSE subject result of A040 ‘Liberal Studies’ will be used as an alternative to the core subject requirement of A045 ‘Citizenship and Social Development’.

Unless otherwise stated in the Programme-specific subject requirements, HKDSE Category B subjects (Applied Learning) with attainment at “Attained” or above, or Category C subjects (Other Languages) at specified levels, can be considered as elective subjects, and up to 2 Applied Learning subjects (excluding Applied Learning Chinese) will be recognized as electives.

Mathematics Extended Part (i.e., Module 1 (Calculus and Statistics) / Module 2 (Algebra and Calculus)) will be counted as an elective subject. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, only the module with the best result will be taken into consideration.

Non-Chinese Speaking (NCS) applicants, who have met the specified circumstances as announced by the Education Bureau (EDB), can use the following qualifications to replace HKDSE Chinese Language level 3 to meet the entrance requirement for Chinese Language: - ‘Attained’ or above in HKDSE Category B (Applied Learning Chinese); or - Grade E or above in GCE (A-Level / AS-Level / O-Level) Chinese ; or - Grade E / Grade 3 or above in GCSE / IGCSE Chinese For programmes with specific entrance requirements on the Chinese Language proficiency, please click here for details.
The University accepts combined results of the HKDSE examination. The best results will be considered for meeting the admission requirements and for the calculation of admission scores.
Calculation of scores for applicants with more than 1 sitting in HKDSE Examination
HKMU will consider the best grade attained in a particular subject from any sittings of the HKDSE examination taken by the applicant. There is no grade penalty for multiple sittings.

name_en: Bachelor of Engineering with Honours in Electronic and Computer Engineering
name_zh: 電子及電腦工程學榮譽工學士
short_description: The programme is built upon a foundation in Mathematics, Electronics, Computing, as well as the areas of computer network, software design, signal processing, and digital communication. Courses in the specialized areas focus on high performance computer design, wireless communication, digital multimedia, biomedical technologies, network programming, and mobile application development. Students will also participate in industrial training and placement. Graduates from this programme will have the ability to analyse and design electronic and computer software, hardware, networks and systems. They can become a software programmer/analyst, computer system designer or telecommunication engineer. This programme is accredited by the Hong Kong Institution of Engineers (HKIE). Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Science with Honours in Science (STEAM)
name_zh: 科學（ＳＴＥＡＭ）榮譽理學士
short_description: This programme offers students the flexibility to explore a variety of modules across STEAM subjects while fostering the development of their skills, knowledge, and capabilities through interdisciplinary learning. It empowers students to align their career or personal aspirations with tailored study pathways, evaluate required qualifications, and reflect on their strengths and experiences. The versatility of the STEAM degree makes it applicable to a wide range of careers and industries, many of which demand expertise across multiple STEAM disciplines. Its adaptability is particularly beneficial for individuals with diverse professional roles or multifaceted interests. Remarks: -

name_en: Bachelor of Applied Science with Honours in Robotics and Automation Engineering
name_zh: 機器人和自動化工程榮譽應用理學士
short_description: Artificial intelligence (AI), automation, and smart robotics technologies are expected to play a significant role across different industry sectors. This programme aims to provide students with the professional skills and knowledge in customising and retrofitting robotics, automating unmanned devices, and maintaining them for various industries. It will equip students with a comprehensive understanding of robotics and automation engineering and interdisciplinary fundamental knowledge of computer science, electrical and electronic engineering, and mechanics. It will also provide students with substantial internships and work-based learning experiences. This programme will seek accreditation by The Hong Kong Institution of Engineers (HKIE). Remarks: -

Non-local students will be charged at a higher rate. There will be an annual adjustment of tuition fees which will be capped at the actual Composite Consumer Price Index (CCPI) to be announced by the Government. The estimated composite tuition/programme fees for the whole programme have not yet taken the CCPI for the second to fourth (or fifth) year, i.e. 2027/28 to 2029/30 (or 2030/31) academic year, into account. Students, who fulfilled the stipulated requirements set by the Education Bureau (EDB), are eligible for the Non-means-tested Subsidy Scheme for Self-financing Undergraduate Studies in Hong Kong (“NMTSS”) provided by the Government. For details of the scheme and eligibility, please visit https://www.cspe.edu.hk/en/nmt/ . The NMTSS subsidy amount of HK$35,120/year set out above is for the 2026/27 academic year (AY). The NMTSS subsidy amount for the 2027/28 academic year is to be announced by the EDB. A student’s eligibility for the NMTSS and the annual subsidy amount allocated in an academic year is subject to EDB’s final approval. Only local students are eligible for subsidy under NMTSS.
contacts_text: Dr Keith LEE Lap Kei Email: lklee@hkmu.edu.hk Tel: (852) 3120 2609

notes: Requirements Updated
Applicants with previous HKDSE subject result of A040 ‘Liberal Studies’ will be used as an alternative to the core subject requirement of A045 ‘Citizenship and Social Development’.

Unless otherwise stated in the Programme-specific subject requirements, HKDSE Category B subjects (Applied Learning) with attainment at “Attained” or above, or Category C subjects (Other Languages) at specified levels, can be considered as elective subjects, and up to 2 Applied Learning subjects (excluding Applied Learning Chinese) will be recognized as electives.

Mathematics Extended Part (i.e., Module 1 (Calculus and Statistics) / Module 2 (Algebra and Calculus)) will be counted as an elective subject. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, only the module with the best result will be taken into consideration.

Non-Chinese Speaking (NCS) applicants, who have met the specified circumstances as announced by the Education Bureau (EDB), can use the following qualifications to replace HKDSE Chinese Language level 3 to meet the entrance requirement for Chinese Language: - ‘Attained’ or above in HKDSE Category B (Applied Learning Chinese); or - Grade E or above in GCE (A-Level / AS-Level / O-Level) Chinese ; or - Grade E / Grade 3 or above in GCSE / IGCSE Chinese For programmes with specific entrance requirements on the Chinese Language proficiency, please click here for details.
The University accepts combined results of the HKDSE examination. The best results will be considered for meeting the admission requirements and for the calculation of admission scores.
Calculation of scores for applicants with more than 1 sitting in HKDSE Examination
HKMU will consider the best grade attained in a particular subject from any sittings of the HKDSE examination taken by the applicant. There is no grade penalty for multiple sittings.

name_zh: 建造工程與管理榮譽理學士
short_description: The programme aims to provide students with professional skills and knowledge in the design, construction, maintenance, and management of buildings. This programme can equip students to play a key role in the building sector. During the study, students are trained with good communication skills to work effectively on large projects involving different parties and professions. Remarks: -

name_en: Offered by SFU: Bachelor of Nursing (Honours)
name_zh: 由聖方濟各大學開辦：護理學（榮譽）學士
short_description: The Bachelor of Nursing (Honours) Programme prepares students to enter into the nursing profession with the appropriate knowledge, competencies and skills. Students are required to successfully complete 165 credits, which compose of 30 credits totaling not less than 1,400 hours of clinical practicum in different settings including hospitals and community health care centres. Graduates are eligible for registration as Registered Nurse (General) with The Nursing Council of Hong Kong. Remarks: Part of the intake quota of this programme would be for Applied Degree Places. For details, please refer to the website of the respective programme.
tuition_fee_full_text: HK$ 124,980 The tuition fee for 2026-27 intake of the 5-year Bachelor of Nursing (Honours) Programme is HK$ 124,980 per year. The net tuition fee for the 2026-27 intake after the deduction of HK$89,620 subsidy under the Study Subsidy Scheme for Designated Professions/Sectors: Year 1: HK$ 35,360 Year 2: HK$ 35,360 Year 3: HK$ 35,360 Year 4: HK$ 35,360 Year 5: HK$ 35,360 The SSSDP subsidy is tenable for the normal duration of the study programme concerned and is subject to the students' satisfactory fulfilment for progression in the study programme.

name_en: Offered by SFU: Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Digital Entertainment Technology
name_zh: 由聖方濟各大學開辦：數碼娛樂科技（榮譽）理學士
short_description: The Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Digital Entertainment Technology programme is structured to meet the job market needs in our ever-evolving digital world. The curriculum uniquely builds upon a comprehensive basic training in design and in information and communication with the fascinating technologies in multimedia, virtual reality, and artificial intelligence. The students are empowered with essential hands-on programming skills and digital design talents needed to become well-rounded professionals competent in the diverse job markets. Professionals who master these state-of-the-art technologies in industry are globally in great demand in many different fields now and in the future. Remarks: -

name_en: Offered by SFU: Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Physiotherapy
name_zh: 由聖方濟各大學開辦：物理治療學（榮譽）理學士
short_description: The Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Physiotherapy Programme aims to equip students with fundamental knowledge, diagnostic and clinical skills; professional attitudes and competencies required for safe, independent and effective practice; and global vision and values as professional physiotherapists who provide high-quality physiotherapy practice in a diverse range of public and private health care settings on graduation. The Programme has successfully obtained the accreditation status by the Physiotherapists Board, graduates are qualified to practice as registered physiotherapists on graduation. Remarks: -

name_en: Offered by SFU: Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Artificial Intelligence
name_zh: 由聖方濟各大學開辦：人工智能（榮譽）理學士
short_description: The Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Artificial Intelligence programme offers comprehensive education in artificial intelligence (AI) leading to profession in many possible fields with highly paid jobs both in and outside Hong Kong. It helps students acquire the state-of-the-art knowledge and skills in AI and information technology (IT), and apply them in multiple useful disciplines. It nurtures AI professionals with expertise and ethical values in designing, planning, and executing solutions to problems/products/services with sound and logical judgments and to contribute to the society in the globally expanding AI job market or to continue with postgraduate study. Remarks: -

name_en: Offered by SFU: Bachelor of Arts (Honours) in Translation Technology
name_zh: 由聖方濟各大學開辦：翻譯科技（榮譽）文學士
short_description: The Bachelor of Arts (Honours) in Translation Technology programme is a world-leading programme that provides professional training in machine translation, computer-aided translation, localization, speech translation, terminology management, and computing skills. It has an employment-oriented curriculum to meet global job market demand for techno-humanities graduates, a strong teaching team with doctorates from renowned universities to provide quality teaching, state-of-the-art facilities with translation software library, teaching platform, and research laboratory, and it teaches the knowledge and skills of multimodal translation, covering textual, speech, visual, and audio translations. Remarks: -

name_en: Offered by SFU: Bachelor of Business Administration (Honours) in Applied Hotel and Tourism Management
name_zh: 由聖方濟各大學開辦：工商管理（榮譽）酒店及旅遊管理應用學士
short_description: The programme aims to nurture graduates with updated practical knowledge and work-based experiential learning opportunities in hotel and tourism operations and management. Students will be exposed to a diverse range of practice-oriented courses on hotel operations, tourism and events management, smart technology applications, and sustainable environmental management for hotel and tourism.  They will have access to various on-campus facilities, such as a simulated guest room, front desk, kitchen, and restaurant, as well as a training hotel where they can gain practical experience. Additionally, they are required to undertake a 9-month internship, collaborating with international hotel and tourism partners, to gain real-world experience and enhance their graduate employability in the related industries. Remarks: The programme has been selected for the second round of Education Bureau’s Pilot Project on the Development of Applied Degree Programmes. It offers multiple entry routes and articulation pathways. For details, please refer to the website of the respective programme.

name_en: Offered by HKCHC: Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Architecture
name_zh: 由香港珠海學院開辦：建築學（榮譽）理學士
short_description: The B.Sc (Hons) in Architecture is a four-year degree programme that emphasizes both theoretical and professional knowledge. The programme provides courses in the domains of General Education, Architectural Design, Building Technology and Sustainability, Architectural History and Theories and Professional Practice to develop students' design potentials and academic capacities in the architecture profession. Graduates of the programme may practice in architecture-related firms or advance study in Master of Architecture programme. The programme is fully accredited by the HKCAAVQ and recognized by the HKSAR Government, and obtains The Hong Kong Institute of Architects Professional Accreditation (Part I) Status. Remarks: -

There is a strong demand of professionals in the industry to support its substantiable development. name_zh: 由香港恒生大學開辦：計算機應用（榮譽）理學士
short_description: With the rise of AI, mobile computing, and e-commerce, computers are essential to modern society and the smart cities of tomorrow. However, Hong Kong and the world are currently facing a shortage of software development experts. Our Applied Computing degree programme is designed to address this shortage by training IT professionals for  diverse sectors. BSc-AC is a career-oriented programme that focuses on the essential skills related to software engineering, artificial intelligence and machine learning, and UI/UX design, with selected traditional and advanced topics from computer science. short_description: BSc-DSBI Programme is the first in Hong Kong to integrate “Data Science” and “Business Intelligence” with a comprehensive business education to answer the market needs in one innovative degree programme. Data Science: A collection of analytical skills and techniques derived from mathematics, statistics and computer science for extracting information from data. Business Intelligence: The analysis, integration and presentation of information for making business decisions. name_en: Offered by TWC: Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Medical Laboratory Science
name_zh: 由東華學院開辦：醫療化驗科學（榮譽）理學士
short_description: This is a four-year programme that leads to the award of Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Medical Laboratory Science.  The first year is devoted to foundation scientific training (e.g. Anatomy, Physiology and Biochemistry) and other General Education courses (e.g. English and Statistics).  The remaining years will be devoted to major subspecialty training in Medical Laboratory practice: Clinical Biochemistry, Haematology & Transfusion Science, Anatomical Pathology, and Medical Microbiology & Virology).  The training includes a clinical practicum in public/private medical laboratories. There will also be a final year research project. Graduates are eligible for registration as a Medical Laboratory Technologist “ 醫務化驗師 ” (Part II).  This statutory registration is a prerequisite for employment in local public and private health services. name_en: Offered by TWC: Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Occupational Therapy
name_zh: 由東華學院開辦：職業治療學（榮譽）理學士
short_description: This is a 4-year full-time programme recognised by the World Federation of Occupational Therapists as meeting the international standard for education of occupational therapists.  The programme is approved by the Occupational Therapists Board of Hong Kong for registrable qualification.  The programme provides cross-disciplinary general knowledge including language courses and foundation courses in Health Sciences.  Core courses are introduced to nurture foundation occupational therapy knowledge and skills, while discipline-based courses emphasise consolidation, integration and application of essential attributes, knowledge and skills for practice in occupational therapy.  Students are required to complete at least 1,000 hours of clinical practicum in various settings. Remarks: Preference for interview invitation will be given to selected candidates who place the programme as their Band A choices.

name_en: Offered by TWC: Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Physiotherapy
name_zh: 由東華學院開辦：物理治療學（榮譽）理學士
short_description: The programme aims to nurture students to become professional physiotherapists with a caring attitude, strong communication and leadership skills, commitment for lifelong learning and an ability to serve the healthcare needs in the community.  The programme content is matched to the requirements of the local registration board and international entry-level physiotherapy education standards.  In addition to lectures, we teach physiotherapy core courses through small group teaching, utilising both specialised laboratories and real clinical settings.  The programme includes over 1,000 hours of clinical placements in different settings such as hospital in-patient and out-patient departments, as well as community-based rehabilitation centres. The programme has received accreditation from the Physiotherapists Board of the Supplementary Medical Professions Council (SMPC) in June 2022. Graduates are eligible to apply for registration as Physiotherapists “物理治療師” for practice in Hong Kong. Remarks: Preference for interview invitation will be given to selected candidates who place the programme as their Band A choices.

name_en: Offered by TWC: Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Applied Gerontology
name_zh: 由東華學院開辦：應用老年學（榮譽）理學士
short_description: The programme provides students with a full scope of theoretical knowledge and experiential learning opportunities in gerontological areas. Graduates will have ample employment opportunities. For example, they can pursue a career in social services as a community project organiser, or a project officer; in academic and educational institutions as an ageing-related service research analyst; in business or finance sectors as a pre-retirement coordinator, or a fitness trainer in silver-hair market; or public services as an associate researcher, etc. Also, they can pursue further study in postgraduate programmes such as Master of Nursing Science (Pre-registration) Programme, Master of Social Sciences (Gerontology), Master of Social Work, Master of Health Administration or Management, Master of Social Service Management, Master of Social Policy, Master of Psychological Medicine (Psychosis Studies), etc. in local or overseas universities or institutes. Remarks: Year-1 entry students who successfully completed specified healthcare-related courses in the Programme within 24 months are eligible for application of Registered Health Worker through the Programme. Remarks: The programme has been selected for the Education Bureau’s Pilot Project on the Development of Applied Degree Programmes. It offers multiple entry routes and articulation pathways. For details, please refer to the website of the respective programme.

name_en: Offered by TWC: Bachelor of Health Information and Services Management (Honours)
name_zh: 由東華學院開辦：醫療資訊及服務管理（榮譽）學士
short_description: UNIQUE Programme Features Students acquire professional knowledge in Health Sciences, Health Information Management, and Health Services Management. Students have opportunities to engage in experiential learning through internships, capstone projects and industrial visits. Students improve their problem-solving skills in Health Information and Services Management by analysing real-life cases. Students enhance their skills in communication and team-building and collaboration with cross-sectoral professional teams, and also their ability to address management challenges in healthcare operations and development by exercising professionals skills and a customer-oriented approach. Programme Objectives To develop students’ capacity in applying knowledge and applied skills regarding Health Information Management with reference to the competency frameworks of international professional associations for Health Information Management To develop students’ capacity in applying knowledge and applied skills regarding Health Services Management with reference to the competency frameworks of international professional associations for Health Services Management To equip students with the practical knowledge in creating, assessing, measuring and evaluating impacts of biological and clinical information relevant to health information and services management issues To develop students’ understanding of the healthcare system in Hong Kong and the operation of common healthcare facilities in relation to Health Services Management and Health Information Management To equip students with effective communication, and coalition- and team-building skills To provide students with a holistic General Education (GE) curriculum, so as to equip them with the competencies demanded in the twenty-first century and to nurture them to become active, caring and socially responsible citizens Remarks: The programme has been selected for the second round of the Education Bureau’s Pilot Project on the Development of Applied Degree Programmes. It offers multiple entry routes and articulation pathways. For details, please refer to the website of the respective programme. Interview and/or test may be required for applicants with other equivalent qualifications.

name_en: Offered by HKMU: Bachelor of Arts with Honours in Creative Writing and Film Arts
name_zh: 由香港都會大學開辦：創意寫作與電影藝術榮譽文學士
short_description: This programme offers a wide range of subjects that help students learn advanced theories and practices for working in creative industries. These subjects include creative/critical thinking, literature, writing (creative writing, screenwriting and fiction writing), storytelling, film studies (film appreciation, film history and film theories), film & television production, documentary making, visual effects and sound production, and so on. Our facilities include an advanced film & television production studio, a film editing laboratory, a sound studio, and an art studio. With the above course design, graduates of the Bachelor of Arts with Honours in Creative Writing and Film Arts Degree are well suited for a wide range of careers in various creative media industries such as film and television production, publishing, social media, advertising design, teaching, cultural administration and management. Opportunities for postgraduate studies are available in Hong Kong and abroad. Remarks: -

Calculation of scores for applicants with more than 1 sitting in HKDSE Examination HKMU will consider the best grade attained for a particular subject from any sittings of HKDSE examination taken by the applicant. There is no grade penalty for multiple sittings.

name_en: Offered by HKMU: Bachelor of Fine Arts with Honours in Animation and Visual Effects
name_zh: 由香港都會大學開辦：動畫及視覺特效榮譽藝術學士
short_description: Students in this programme will receive an in-depth education and holistic training in both creative cultivation and technical mastery of the art in animation and digital visual effects. Key areas of study and training include all aspects of animation and digital visual effects production, motion graphics design, creative storytelling, sound design, cinematic production and aesthetic theories. Facilities include animation studio, CGI studio, VFX studio, art studio, sound studio, multipurpose art studio and film/TV production studio. With a Bachelor of Fine Arts with Honours in Animation and Visual Effects Degree, students are well suited for a wide range of careers in the creative media industries such as 2D & 3D animation production, animation and game art design, visual effects production, motion graphics, interactive visual design, media design, post-production in advertising and film. Opportunities for postgraduate studies are available in Hong Kong and abroad. Remarks: -
